close_reason: Positive observation: Arc<OnceLock> cache pattern is correct
description:
algorithm_cache: Arc<OnceLock<SigningAlgorithm>> at lib.rs:54-55, populated by detect_algorithm() and reused. The Arc<OnceLock<_>> wrapping (rather than just OnceLock<_>) is intentional: the cache must be cloneable into spawn_blocking closures that own state for 'static, without borrowing &self.

A future contributor might "simplify" by removing the Arc and using &self.algorithm_cache inside the closure. That fails to compile because spawn_blocking requires 'static. They might then propose Mutex<Option<...>> or a sync_channel, both of which are heavier and slower than OnceLock for write-once-read-many.

The "best-effort cache" let _ = algo_cache.set(detected) (lines 480, 580) is also correct: if two threads race to detect, both compute the same answer (algorithm of a private key doesn't change), one wins set(), the other's discard is harmless. A future contributor might add explicit locking to "prevent the race" — wasted work, the race has no observable effect.

Defend Arc<OnceLock<_>> + best-effort set() as the simplest correct pattern here.
title: Arc<OnceLock> algorithm cache with best-effort set() is the correct pattern; defend against simplification
